EventFixtureSeed.description: A tests/fixtures/events/ fixture file: versioned envelope (DC5) around one Event plus DC4 metadata (shared SeedValueMetadata shape) for every numeric leaf under `event`. Test-only — never loaded by runtime code and never part of the /data seed-discovery path (EVT1 asserts data/events does not exist).

triggers.description: ALL must hold against the observed state to become ELIGIBLE (Doc 15 §3).

outcomes.description: Resolution branches; exactly one is selected deterministically at ACTIVE → RESOLVING.

chain_next.description: Literal null: multi-step chains are FUTURE BUILD (EVT10) — the schema rejects any non-null value.

invariant_refs.description: EVT + any suite this event touches (Doc 15 §3).

Event.description: A data-defined event (Doc 15 §3 at A3 scope). At A3 these exist ONLY as schema-validated test fixtures under tests/fixtures/events/ — no real /data event content, no runtime content loading (A3 brief §2).

EventClass.description: Event taxonomy (Doc 15 §1/§3) — pure classification labels; the A3 lifecycle is class-agnostic.

kind.description: A1 harbor spine observation: the named band of a CoreResource is at least `amount`.
A2 Claim Ledger observation: global non-story package occupancy is at most `count` (Doc 04 §5 slots).
Prior-event completion: the referenced event id appears in the observed resolved set.

Condition.description: Trigger conditions (EVT4): a CLOSED union over observable state that exists at A3 — nothing else is expressible. Every numeric threshold in a fixture carries DC4 metadata (the fixture envelope enforces coverage).

CoreResource.description: The four storable, spendable, cap-bound resources (Doc 07 §2, D26). Storage, exposed surplus, cargo, and raid loss type against CoreResource ONLY (DC6) — never StandingResource or ReceiptMetric.

ObservableBand.description: Storage-band selector for harbor-observing conditions ("total" = safe + exposed).

OfferWindow.description: Offer-window reference (Doc 15 §3 `offer_window: Timer | "imposed"`). At A3 no clock exists: `window_ref` is a reference label, and expiry is an explicit harness-supplied signal. Real timer mechanics are FUTURE BUILD (EVT7/D35 fairness doctrine binds when they exist).

Objective.description: Structural objective reference (Doc 15 §3). Inert at A3 — completion is a harness-supplied signal, not evaluated gameplay.

Outcome.description: One resolution branch (Doc 15 §3): the effects staged if this outcome is selected at ACTIVE → RESOLVING.

Effect.description: Inert effect descriptor (Doc 15 §4): names the existing system it would route through. Carries NO amounts, NO payload, NO execution path — it is staged and preserved by the lifecycle, never applied (A3 brief §2).

EffectBinding.description: The existing system an effect WOULD bind to (Doc 15 §4 table). Pure reference vocabulary at A3: no dispatch to any of these systems exists, and the lifecycle never interprets this field (EVT5/EVT6 fail-loud).

SeedValueMetadata.description: DC4 unit-requirement metadata for one numeric seed value. `path` is the dot-path of the numeric field under the seed's payload root (`kit` for guardian seeds, `storage` for resource-storage seeds); the validator enforces that every numeric leaf has exactly one entry and every entry resolves to a numeric leaf.

Unit.description: Value-cadence vocabulary for seed metadata (Doc 07 §2 / §1.2, DC4).

$comment: GENERATED from src/contracts/event.ts (D39) — do not edit by hand; run `pnpm run schema:generate`.
